evidence: src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts keeps module-level mutable state (`let errorNotifier`) and exposes `configureErrorNotifier`, so UI wiring is injected into a shared error hub used broadly by feature hooks.

src/integrations/supabase/client.ts keeps module-level singleton state (`instrumentationInstalled`, `clientInstance`) and exports a `Proxy` (`supabase`) that lazily bootstraps runtime on property access, making boundary behavior implicit.

src/types/generationAndShots.ts is a canonical barrel spanning generation + shot types, and feature hooks (e.g., src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts) consume it directly, increasing central fan-in around cross-domain shared entry points.

Mobile/touch interaction ownership is split across multiple layers: generic UI primitive behavior in src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx (`TouchableTooltip`), page-specific custom tooltip state logic in src/pages/Home/components/CreativePartnerPane.tsx, and gallery-level gesture orchestration in src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMobileInteractions.ts.

Installation UX is decomposed into scenario mapping (src/pages/Home/components/installInstructions/scenarios.ts) and visual registry (src/pages/Home/components/installInstructions/visuals.tsx), but the visual module is large and tightly literalized, reducing clarity of ownership boundaries for future variants.

Some shared modules are clean single-purpose facades (src/shared/lib/utils.ts, src/shared/lib/toolConstants.ts), but the architecture still relies on high-fan-in hubs and implicit globals for core concerns (error and client runtime).
src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useButtonGroupProps.ts builds four prop objects that mostly copy fields verbatim from `shared` + input objects, adding almost no translation logic.

src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts defines a very large `UseImageEditValueParams` bag and then splits/re-merges it through `buildModeState`/`buildCanvasAndAnnotationState`/`buildRepositionAndPanelState`/`buildFormAndGenerationState`, creating layering overhead without reducing coupling.

Task creation modules (src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts) repeat the same validate -> build params -> create task orchestration pattern with tool-specific naming.

src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts and src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts each define their own `TaskDbRow` + snake_case->camelCase mapper; integration seams for task data are duplicated instead of funneled through a single boundary translator.

Travel segment parsing/normalization helpers (`toRecordOrEmpty`, `asNumber`) are duplicated across src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts and src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts, signaling drift-prone seam logic.

src/shared/imageEditCore/utils.ts re-exports `downloadMedia` from src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/utils, so a lower-level shared/core path depends on a component-layer utility through an alias seam.
src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts has both class API and a full forwarding facade object (`dataFreshnessManager`) with one wrapper per method, adding repetitive low-value boilerplate.

src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts and src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts rely on very large object construction with extensive field-by-field forwarding, making local edits noisy and error-prone.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useButtonGroupProps.ts repeats the same shared-field projection in each builder function (`isVideo`, `readOnly`, `isSpecialEditMode`, `selectedProjectId`, `isCloudMode`).

`buildTravelRequestBody` accepts both grouped structs (`motionParams`, `generationParams`, `seedParams`) and a parallel set of flat legacy fields in the same public function signature, then merges them via fallback logic (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:463-543`).

`invalidateGenerationsSync` is named as synchronous but can defer work with `setTimeout`, while sibling invalidation API `invalidateVariantChange` is explicitly async/await and returns `Promise<void>` (`src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:84-113`).

The same module exposes both `useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration` and a backward-compatible alias `useGetTaskIdForGeneration`, expanding equivalent entry points instead of converging callers (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts:115-129`).

Multiple small utility modules carry long narrative headers and restating comments that mirror code behavior rather than explaining non-obvious constraints (e.g., src/shared/lib/storagePaths.ts, src/shared/lib/taskTypeUtils.ts, src/shared/lib/taskCreation/arrayUtils.ts).
Several comments describe obvious one-line behavior ('Get all visible task types', 'Generate a unique thumbnail filename'), increasing scan noise relative to executable logic in these shared files.
ai_generated_debt.unreported_risk: If this comment-heavy pattern continues in shared utilities, maintenance cost rises through slower code reading and higher review noise even without functional regressions.

evidence: Travel task input type intentionally mixes canonical and legacy payload shapes: TravelBetweenImagesTaskParamsInput = TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams & Partial<LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParamsInput> (src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts).

Legacy guidance fields are still carried in active UI-facing config type (motion_strength, structure_type, uni3c_* comments marked migration-only) in src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts.

Image generation settings still accept deprecated fields via LegacyProjectImageSettings and ProjectImageSettingsInput union, while canonical fields coexist in the same boundary type (src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/types.ts).
Task config and ID helpers include compatibility fallbacks for old naming/registry paths (src/shared/lib/taskConfig.ts, src/shared/lib/taskIdHelpers.ts).

All assigned root configs are marked peripheral in holistic root_files: vitest.edge.smoke.config.ts (fan_in 1, fan_out 1), vitest.edge.config.ts (fan_in 0, fan_out 1), vitest.config.ts (fan_in 0, fan_out 0), vite.config.ts (fan_in 0, fan_out 0), tailwind.config.ts (fan_in 0, fan_out 0).

vitest.edge.smoke.config.ts and vitest.edge.config.ts are thin re-export wrappers over supabase/functions configs, indicating split ownership across root and supabase/functions without a focused config package.

src/shared/hooks/ and src/shared/components/ are large catch-all directories (95 and 78 files respectively) while containing heterogeneous concerns in assigned files (API token persistence, generic async orchestration, autosave state machine, LoRA UI, app error boundary, aspect-ratio selector).

Large orchestration hooks centralize multiple concerns instead of a single cohesive responsibility: `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useImageEditOrchestrator.ts` (541 lines) composes mode hooks, persistence sync, and context shaping in one unit, with a very large dependency list for one `useMemo` context object (~lines 392-520).

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts` (706 lines) combines variants, navigation, star state, references, join clips, lineage, shot management, and layout composition in one API surface (~lines 602-620).

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/useTimelineOrchestrator.ts` (663 lines) mixes drag, zoom, selection, drop pipeline, endpoint dragging, and structure-video placement logic in one hook (~lines 141-577).

`supabase/functions/complete_task/index.ts` runs a 15-step flow (auth, rate limit, ownership, storage, params mutation, generation creation, orchestrator completion, billing) in one handler (~lines 142-353), indicating mixed responsibilities at the function boundary.

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/controllers/useGenerationController.ts` uses a very large parameter object and mostly forwards it to collaborator hooks (~lines 67-220), and `useEditingController.ts` similarly aggregates and re-exports many unrelated concerns (~lines 39-224).

`src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx`, and `src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` use a semicolon-free/double-quote style, while nearby shared modules such as `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` and `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` use single quotes + semicolons.

`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/index.tsx` exports both named and default (`export { MediaGallery }; export default MediaGallery;`) while many sibling shared components in this batch only expose named exports, creating mixed import conventions.

`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` defines a normalized AppError path, but callers in `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` and `src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts` still throw raw `Error`/Supabase errors directly in multiple paths.
`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` logs actionable errors with `console.error` and returns, bypassing shared error normalization and telemetry context.

`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` and `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts` use sentinel returns (`null`/early return) plus console logging in failure branches, while other task modules (`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts`) catch then rethrow after `handleError`, yielding mixed failure contracts.
`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts` applies explicit auth gates via `authenticateRequest(...)` and `auth.isServiceRole` before privileged service-role DB operations.

Client/routes files in scope (`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`, `src/app/routes.tsx`) do not introduce obvious hardcoded credential bypasses; protected tool routes are centralized behind `Layout` per in-file comments.
authorization_consistency.unreported_risk: Auth trust boundaries are partly implicit in comments and entrypoint wiring; internal function modules in this batch do not self-assert auth assumptions, so future refactors could accidentally expose privileged paths without immediate local signal.

findings.summary: Shared error handling uses module-global notifier state, creating hidden coupling between UI notification wiring and cross-feature error flows.
evidence: `handleError.ts` defines `let errorNotifier` and `configureErrorNotifier`, then conditionally emits UI-facing notification payloads in `handleError`.
Feature hooks (`useMediaGalleryHandlers`, `useMediaGalleryItemState`) call `handleError(...)` directly, so their behavior depends on external global notifier registration order.
This introduces an implicit cross-module contract not visible in function signatures at call sites.
findings.suggestion: Replace global notifier mutation with explicit boundary injection: keep `handleError` pure (normalize/log/return), and perform toast dispatch in an app-level adapter (or context) at composition boundaries.

findings.summary: Supabase client access is hidden behind a proxy-backed singleton with global window reuse/debug mutation, obscuring runtime boundaries.
evidence: `client.ts` stores global mutable singleton state and installs instrumentation once, then initializes via `Proxy` traps on property reads/writes (`get`, `set`, `ownKeys`, etc.).
`initializeSupabaseDebugGlobals` mutates `window.__supabase_client__` and `window.supabase`, adding another implicit global channel.
Like `handleError`'s notifier global, this pattern favors hidden runtime wiring over explicit dependency boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Deprecate proxy export in favor of explicit `getSupabaseClient()` usage across call sites; isolate debug-global mutation behind a dedicated dev-only module invoked from app bootstrap.

findings.summary: Touch/tooltip behavior is implemented in several layers with overlapping responsibilities, reducing architectural coherence.
evidence: `tooltip.tsx` includes `TouchableTooltip` with custom touch toggling, outside-tap closing, and hover guards.
`useMobileInteractions.ts` separately implements double-tap timing and outside/scroll close behavior for gallery overlays.
`CreativePartnerPane.tsx` reimplements mobile tooltip toggling/disable logic directly for ecosystem tooltip state.
findings.suggestion: Define one shared interaction policy layer (e.g., reusable touch-overlay controller hook) and consume it from tooltip/gallery/home components instead of maintaining parallel gesture/state semantics.

findings.summary: Button-group prop builders add indirection but almost no transformation, increasing abstraction cost.
evidence: All four builders in useButtonGroupProps.ts copy the same `shared` fields plus direct pass-through input callbacks/flags.
The memoization key is `[params]`, so the wrapper object itself still re-computes whenever parent passes a new params object.
Consumers in LightboxLayout and render-model code use these objects as plain prop bags; no boundary validation or schema adaptation is applied.
findings.suggestion: Collapse this into a single typed `sharedProps` spread at call sites (or one small helper) and keep only builders that perform real translation/normalization.

findings.summary: Task DB-to-domain mapping is duplicated across bridge and hook layers, creating seam drift risk.
evidence: generationTaskBridge.ts defines `TaskDbRow` and `mapDbTaskRowToTask` locally.
useTasks.ts defines another `TaskDbRow` and `mapDbTaskToTask` with the same field mapping pattern.
Multiple task-related hooks/mutations consume task-shaped data, so schema changes must be synchronized manually across seam copies.
findings.suggestion: Extract one canonical task mapper + DB row type in a shared task adapter module and import it from both bridge and hooks.

findings.summary: Travel task modules duplicate JSON normalization helpers and segment payload shaping logic.
evidence: individualTravelSegment.ts and payloadBuilder.ts both define local `toRecordOrEmpty` and numeric/string coercion helpers.
VideoTravelDetails.tsx independently re-implements similar unknown-to-typed extraction utilities (`asRecordOrUndefined`, `asString`, `asNumber`, arrays).
These seams all parse the same travel/orchestrator payload families, but each boundary has its own ad-hoc coercion rules.
findings.suggestion: Create shared travel-param parsing utilities (or schema-based validators) used by task builders and details rendering to keep one normalization contract.

findings.summary: A shared/core utility file re-exports a component-layer helper, inverting dependency direction.
evidence: src/shared/imageEditCore/utils.ts exports `downloadMedia` from `@/shared/components/MediaLightbox/utils`.
useInlineEditState.ts imports `downloadMedia` from `imageEditCore/utils`, so edit-core depends indirectly on MediaLightbox component code.
This blurs ownership between reusable edit core and UI-specific lightbox implementation.
findings.suggestion: Move `downloadMedia` into a neutral shared lib utility (non-component path) and have both imageEditCore and MediaLightbox import from that base module.

findings.summary: Inline image edit state assembly uses a very large param bag with repeated field forwarding, reducing local clarity.
evidence: UseImageEditValueParams in useImageEditValue.ts contains a high number of fields spanning mode, canvas, forms, transforms, status, and callbacks.
The hook then decomposes and recomposes these fields via four build functions that mostly forward values unchanged.
useInlineEditState.ts must construct and thread this large bag, increasing editing friction and coupling across hook boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Split image edit context into smaller domain slices (mode/canvas/generation), pass structured sub-objects, and reduce flat forwarding APIs.

findings.summary: `buildTravelRequestBody` exposes two overlapping API shapes in one signature
evidence: `buildTravelRequestBody` takes both modern grouped params and legacy flat params, then resolves precedence dynamically (`generateVideoService.ts:463-543`).

The module also exports internal/test-focused helpers and compatibility aliases, increasing callable surface area with overlapping semantics (`generateVideoService.ts:591-613`, `generationTaskBridge.ts:124-129`).
findings.suggestion: Split into two explicit entry points: a strict modern `buildTravelRequestBodyV2(input)` and a thin deprecated adapter for legacy callers. Remove dual-field fallback logic from the core builder and migrate call sites to one canonical shape.

findings.summary: Invalidation APIs mix sync naming with async behavior
evidence: `invalidateGenerationsSync` can schedule delayed invalidation with `setTimeout`, so effects are asynchronous despite a sync name (`useGenerationInvalidation.ts:84-93`).
Sibling APIs in the same module return `Promise<void>` and await refetches (`invalidateVariantChange`), creating mixed call expectations (`useGenerationInvalidation.ts:105-141`).
`timelineWriteQueue` APIs show explicit async contracts for queued/delayed operations, highlighting inconsistency with invalidation naming (`timelineWriteQueue.ts:108-190`).
findings.suggestion: Normalize the invalidation API to explicit async semantics: rename `invalidateGenerationsSync` to `invalidateGenerations` (or `scheduleGenerationInvalidation`) and return a promise/cancel handle for delayed paths so callers can reason about completion consistently.

findings.summary: Task/generation translation rules are duplicated across seam modules
evidence: Primary task selection (`tasks[0]`) is implemented in multiple boundary translators instead of one shared policy (`generationTaskBridge.ts:77-103`, `generationTransformers.ts:237-243`).

Shot-generation mapping logic is re-authored in service orchestration (`buildImagePayload`, timeline pair config) rather than consuming a single canonical transformer (`generateVideoService.ts:246-376`).
This creates multiple seam-specific mappers that can drift when DB/query shape changes, requiring synchronized edits across modules.
findings.suggestion: Create a shared boundary-mapper module for generation/task and shot-generation normalization (e.g., `generationBoundaryMappers.ts`) and make `generationTaskBridge`, `generationTransformers`, and `generateVideoService` consume it. Keep orchestration modules focused on flow control, not schema translation.

findings.summary: Shared utility files contain excessive restating comments that add noise without new intent.
evidence: Large header/doc blocks describe straightforward code paths and repeat symbol names/behavior directly visible from implementation.
Comment density is high relative to logic size in these helpers, reducing signal-to-noise during maintenance.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to constraint-focused notes only (why a behavior exists, migration/removal conditions, external contract caveats) and remove line-by-line narration of obvious code.

findings.summary: Core task-setting boundaries still accept legacy and canonical fields simultaneously.
evidence: TravelBetweenImagesTaskParamsInput and ProjectImageSettingsInput both merge modern contracts with legacy/deprecated fields.
Legacy-only metadata fields remain embedded in active config interfaces, not isolated to a dedicated decode/migration layer.
Compatibility fallbacks remain active in task config and ID resolution, signaling migration not yet closed.
findings.suggestion: Move legacy decoding into explicit adapter functions at ingestion boundaries, keep runtime/domain types canonical-only, and enforce a removal gate by rejecting legacy fields after cutover.

findings.summary: Two near-duplicate aspect-ratio helpers create avoidable low-level divergence risk.
evidence: Both modules compute aspectRatio strings with similar project-ratio fallback and square default behavior.
One implementation additionally reads metadata/orchestrator resolution while the other only parses project ratio, indicating fragmented logic ownership.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate into one shared aspect-ratio resolver with optional metadata inputs, and have both call sites consume the same helper to prevent drift.

findings.summary: Root-level config files are low-fan-in stragglers and include thin test-config pass-throughs that should be grouped into a dedicated config package.
evidence: Holistic root_files marks all five assigned root configs as peripheral with fan_in < 5 (mostly 0-1).
vitest.edge.smoke.config.ts and vitest.edge.config.ts only re-export supabase/functions vitest configs, creating split placement between root and supabase/functions.
tailwind.config.ts is large (743 LOC) but still root-peripheral with fan_in 0, making root navigation noisier without cross-package reuse justification.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) Create target folders `config/build/` and `config/test/`. 2) Move `vite.config.ts` and `tailwind.config.ts` to `config/build/`; move `vitest.config.ts`, `vitest.edge.config.ts`, and `vitest.edge.smoke.config.ts` to `config/test/`. 3) Update script/tool references to explicit config paths and keep compatibility stubs only if CI requires them. 4) Run import/update checks with `rg -n "vitest\.edge\.config|vitest\.config|vite\.config|tailwind\.config" .` and validate with `vitest --config config/test/vitest.config.ts`, `vitest --config config/test/vitest.edge.config.ts`, and `vite build --config config/build/vite.config.ts`.

findings.summary: `src/shared/hooks` and `src/shared/components` currently mix domain features with generic utilities, weakening capability-based ownership boundaries.
evidence: `useApiTokens.ts` is feature-specific (PAT management and Supabase edge functions) but lives in generic shared hooks.
`useAutoSaveSettings.ts` implements a substantial persistence lifecycle for tool settings (shot/project/custom modes), indicating settings-subsystem ownership rather than global shared placement.

`ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx` and `AspectRatioSelector.tsx` are model/generation UI behavior mixed into general shared components, while holistic directory profiles already show high volume and broad coupling in shared directories.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) Establish target folders `src/features/api-tokens/hooks/`, `src/features/settings/hooks/`, `src/features/generation/components/`, and reserve `src/shared/*` for reusable primitives only. 2) Move feature-specific hooks first (`useApiTokens`, `useAutoSaveSettings`), then domain components (`ActiveLoRAsDisplay`, `AspectRatioSelector`), and keep `AppErrorBoundary` in `shared/components` as app-shell infrastructure. 3) Update imports incrementally with `rg -l "@/shared/hooks/use(ApiTokens|AutoSaveSettings|AsyncOperation)" src` and `rg -l "@/shared/components/(ActiveLoRAsDisplay|AspectRatioSelector)" src`, then rewrite paths. 4) Validate with `tsc --noEmit` and targeted tests `vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useApiTokens.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAutoSaveSettings.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAsyncOperation.test.ts`.

findings.summary: Supabase client boundary keeps two competing contracts and hidden global state coupling.
evidence: client.ts keeps both `getSupabaseClient()` and Proxy `supabase` export for backward compatibility (lines 34-47, 83-87), without an enforced migration endpoint.
client.ts reads/writes `window.__supabase_client__` and `window.supabase` (lines 12-17, 53-60), making behavior depend on mutable ambient globals.
README.md has architecture doc links but no runtime compatibility policy section to govern when shim contracts are removed.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a staged contract consolidation: Stage 1 add a short compatibility policy section in README.md (target folders: root docs + `src/integrations/supabase/`) naming `getSupabaseClient()` as canonical and deprecating `supabase` Proxy; Stage 2 update imports to the canonical API with `rg "import \{ supabase \}" src` then replace call sites; Stage 3 remove Proxy/global debug assignment paths from client.ts and keep only explicit opt-in diagnostics; Stage 4 validate with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test -- src/integrations/supabase`, and `npm run lint`.

findings.summary: High-fan-in shared contracts mix responsibilities and widen blast radius of changes.
evidence: handleError.ts orchestrates normalization, logging, optional notifier dispatch, and callback extension in one global helper (lines 41-56) and is reported as a 226-importer hub.
generationAndShots.ts is a 206-importer type barrel for a specific domain subset, making cross-feature type dependency converge through a single broad alias.
button.tsx is a 155-importer primitive that now embeds retro-policy adaptation logic (lines 57-70), expanding a core UI contract with style-policy concerns.
findings.suggestion: Split ownership contracts by concern using a staged plan: Stage 1 create target folders `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/core/` and `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/ui/`, plus `src/types/generation/` for domain barrels; Stage 2 move notifier-specific behavior from `handleError.ts` into UI-scoped adapter and keep core error pipeline pure; Stage 3 move retro-policy adaptation behind a dedicated wrapper component in `src/shared/components/ui/retro/` while keeping `button.tsx` core-only; Stage 4 update imports with `rg "generationAndShots|handleError|retroButtonPolicy" src` and apply path rewrites; Stage 5 validate with `npm run typecheck` and targeted tests `npm run test -- src/shared/lib/errorHandling src/shared/components/ui`.

findings.summary: A flattened shared/lib plus generic utils barrel obscures package ownership and coupling intent.
evidence: holistic_context.structure shows `src/shared/lib/` has 71 production files across unrelated concerns, passing the mixed-concern overload threshold.
`src/shared/lib/utils.ts` exposes only two exports but uses a broad, catch-all filename, making import intent weak.
button.tsx imports `cn` via `@/shared/lib/utils` (line 4), while coupling matrix shows `src/shared/components/ui/ → src/shared/lib/` at 36 edges, indicating directory-boundary friction.
findings.suggestion: Execute a staged reorg: Stage 1 create target folders `src/shared/lib/ui/`, `src/shared/lib/text/`, and `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/` index files; Stage 2 move `uiClassNames.ts` to `src/shared/lib/ui/classNames.ts` and `stringFormatting.ts` to `src/shared/lib/text/stringFormatting.ts`; Stage 3 replace `src/shared/lib/utils.ts` with explicit re-exports from domain indexes or remove it; Stage 4 update imports in move order (`src/shared/components/ui/*` first, then broader shared/hooks/components) using `rg "@/shared/lib/utils|uiClassNames|stringFormatting" src` plus codemod/search-replace; Stage 5 validate with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test -- src/shared/components/ui src/shared/lib`, and `npm run lint`.

findings.summary: Image edit orchestration hook mixes composition, state syncing, and context assembly into one oversized control surface.
evidence: `useImageEditOrchestrator` takes a broad prop contract spanning media/project info, variant state, persistence state, LoRA setup, and UI refs (~lines 32-92, 128-145).

It composes multiple mode subsystems (`useInpainting`, `useMagicEditMode`, `useRepositionMode`, `useImg2ImgMode`) and then flattens almost all state/actions into a single context value (~lines 176-382, 392-520).
Placeholder context fields (`isFlippedHorizontally = false`, `isSaving = false`) suggest the abstraction boundary is carrying fields it does not own (~lines 388-391).
findings.suggestion: Split by mode orchestration boundaries (e.g., inpaint/magic, reposition, img2img) and build a thin composition layer that only chooses active mode contracts; move context shaping into smaller typed builders per mode.

findings.summary: Timeline orchestrator combines unrelated interaction systems (drag, zoom, drop, endpoint edit, video browser) in one hook.
evidence: Single hook owns local UI state, drag freeze mechanics, zoom center math, deferred rerender timers, drop interceptors, duplicate collision resolution, and structure video placement (~lines 163-540).
Timing-based synchronization (`setTimeout` for rerender and drag end/zoom recentering) is embedded with domain behavior (~lines 340-343, 356-387).
The return contract exposes a very broad API surface (refs, dimensions, drag/drop/selection/zoom handlers, pending states, device info), increasing coupling for consumers (~lines 54-139, 577-620).
findings.suggestion: Decompose into dedicated coordinators (`useTimelineViewport`, `useTimelineDnD`, `useTimelineDropPipeline`, `useStructureVideoPlacement`) with an integration hook that only merges already-cohesive outputs.

findings.summary: Shot editor controllers behave mostly as pass-through aggregators with oversized parameter and return surfaces.
evidence: `useGenerationController` accepts dozens of fields (~lines 7-65) and forwards most directly into `useGenerateBatch` with little local transformation (~lines 155-220).
`useEditingController` aggregates structure video, audio, naming, and join configuration, then returns a wide flattened object (~lines 39-224).
This creates broad call signatures that force unrelated updates to cross the same controller boundary.
findings.suggestion: Introduce scenario-specific controller inputs (`batchGenerationConfig`, `joinConfig`, `structureMediaConfig`) and return grouped sub-objects instead of flattened exports; keep each controller scoped to one user workflow.

findings.summary: Edge handler combines policy, storage, generation side-effects, orchestration, and billing in a single sequential function.
evidence: `completeTaskHandler` executes many distinct concerns inline (request parse, auth/rate limit, ownership, storage verification/upload, task params mutation, generation creation, task completion update, orchestrator completion, billing) (~lines 142-353).
`generation-handlers.ts` is explicitly an all-handlers file and contains variant-on-source, variant-on-parent, variant-on-child, and standalone generation logic in one module (~lines 1-406).
Cross-cutting orchestration logic is split across entrypoint + handlers + orchestrator module with shared task param shape assumptions, increasing change ripple risk.
findings.suggestion: Refactor into an explicit completion pipeline with isolated stages (authorization, storage resolution, generation mutation, orchestrator mutation, billing), each with typed stage input/output and failure policy.

findings.summary: Tasks pane component mixes pane shell behavior, data orchestration, mutation logic, filter state, and lightbox wiring in one file.
evidence: `TasksPane.tsx` (610 lines) owns session persistence, multiple filters/pagination, optimistic cancellation cache mutation, sliding pane behavior, and `MediaLightbox` portal rendering (~lines 59-603).

`TaskList.tsx` and `TaskItem.tsx` add their own local orchestration layers (transition state, new-task detection, optimistic cancellation, navigation/lightbox behavior), indicating responsibilities are distributed by convenience rather than bounded feature seams.
Pane and task domain logic are tightly coupled through large prop surfaces (`TaskList` + `TaskItem` props include navigation, lightbox openers, mobile interaction state, project indicators).
findings.suggestion: Split into container/presenter layers: isolate query+mutation state into hooks (`useTasksPaneFilters`, `useTasksPaneMutations`) and keep pane shell + lightbox host as separate components with narrower prop contracts.

findings.summary: Prompt submission queue paths repeat similar orchestration patterns instead of sharing a single queue execution strategy.
evidence: `handleUseExistingPrompts` and `handleNewPromptsLikeExisting` both run: task param creation, submit trigger, runIncomingTask envelope, and generate call with similar scaffolding (~lines 58-87 and 88-147).

`useSubmissionHandlers` and `useFormSubmission` layer additional wrapper hooks that mostly pass through the same dependencies, increasing indirection without reducing surface complexity (~useSubmissionHandlers lines 24-86; useFormSubmission lines 20-109).
Guard/queue/report behavior is duplicated at multiple levels, making flow changes error-prone.
findings.suggestion: Create a shared `runQueuedSubmission` helper that owns trigger/label/error envelope and accepts only strategy callbacks (build prompts, build params, execute generation).

findings.summary: Shared AppError handling is bypassed by mixed throw/log/return-null patterns, producing inconsistent failure behavior across similar task operations.
evidence: `handleError` normalizes + logs errors, but `useGenerationMutations` throws `new Error(...)` and raw `variantError` directly (lines 47, 97, 115, 133, 137).

`generationTaskBridge` throws new errors in data fetchers (lines 74, 95), but also catches and only calls `handleError` in background preload loop (line 184), creating differing propagation semantics.
`orchestrator.ts` logs and returns `null` in DB error branches (e.g., line 215) while `generation.ts` catches/logs then rethrows (line 143), so callers cannot rely on one error contract.
findings.suggestion: Define and enforce one boundary contract per layer: UI/task helpers either return a typed `Result` or throw normalized `AppError` consistently, and remove ad-hoc `console.error` + sentinel return paths unless explicitly typed/documented.

findings.summary: Several paths suppress or downscope errors, reducing diagnosability and creating hidden failure modes.
evidence: `useDiscordSignIn` intentionally swallows `localStorage` write failures with an empty catch (lines 10-12), losing telemetry about sign-in flow preconditions.
`useMediaGalleryHandlers` emits `console.error` for missing shot/fullShot then returns (lines 59-66), without shared error context or user-level fallback.
`orchestrator.ts` logs DB query errors and returns `null` (line 215), which can collapse distinct infra/query failures into a generic 'not found/no segments' behavior upstream.
findings.suggestion: Route these branches through a lightweight normalized error reporter (even when non-fatal), and distinguish expected-empty vs query-failed via explicit discriminated return types instead of nullable sentinels.

findings.summary: A style island in shared UI/hooks and mixed export conventions create avoidable convention drift in high-import modules.
evidence: UI/hook files use double quotes + no semicolons (`button.tsx`, `tooltip.tsx`, `useMobile.tsx`), while adjacent shared infra/mutation modules use single quotes + semicolons (`useGenerationMutations.ts`).
`MediaGallery/index.tsx` publishes both default and named exports (lines 642-643), while many sibling components in this batch expose only named exports, producing inconsistent import habits.
findings.suggestion: Apply one formatter/lint profile consistently to shared modules and choose one export convention per component category (prefer named-only or documented dual-export policy) to reduce import/style entropy.

findings.summary: Complete-task generation helpers mix null-return, silent logging, and thrown exceptions for comparable DB failure classes.
evidence: generation-core.ts returns null for lookup/linking failures (lines 33-42, 155-160) but throws on insert/create failures (lines 88-90, 132-134).
generation-parent.ts returns null across many catch/fallback branches (for example lines 73-76, 84-87, 95-98, 157-160), forcing ambiguous caller handling.
generation-handlers.ts also logs and returns null in variant paths, compounding mixed contracts.
findings.suggestion: Define an explicit result contract for complete_task helpers (for example Result<T, ErrorCode>), convert current null branches to typed error results, and keep throwing only at top-level request boundary where HTTP status mapping is centralized.

findings.summary: Sibling edge handlers expose inconsistent response construction and error-shape conventions.
evidence: tasks-list and revoke-pat consistently use jsonResponse helper.
delete-project manually builds Response payloads and headers in many branches, duplicating format logic and using a different catch style.
findings.suggestion: Standardize all function entrypoints on _shared/http.ts helpers, add shared typed error payload shape, and codemod manual Response branches to helper calls to keep API surface behavior uniform.

findings.summary: ShotEditor controller layers add heavy indirection with oversized parameter bags and mostly pass-through logic.
evidence: useGenerationController.ts defines >50 config fields in UseGenerationControllerParams and forwards most to useGenerateBatch with minimal transformation.
useEditingController.ts aggregates many hook returns and re-exports them almost verbatim.
useShotEditorController.ts coordinates many controller outputs and prop bridges, making caller/callee boundaries difficult to reason about.
findings.suggestion: Refactor to a small set of cohesive domain configs (promptConfig, motionConfig, joinConfig, persistenceConfig), push transformation to one boundary adapter, and collapse pure forwarding controllers into the consuming hook where no policy is added.

findings.summary: Legacy segment metadata formats are still handled in production hot paths across frontend and edge processing.
evidence: segmentSettingsMigration.ts supports multiple historical schemas (pair_*, user_overrides, additional_loras, new segmentOverrides).
segmentSettingsMigrationLegacy.ts keeps explicit legacy-only metadata fields as active type surface.
complete_task generation flows still carry legacy parent/structure-video fallback logic.
findings.suggestion: Set a removal gate by telemetry (count legacy-field reads/writes), run a migration sweep to canonical segmentOverrides-only payloads, then delete legacy fallback branches and legacy type exports in one coordinated release.

findings.summary: Large shared buckets combine mixed concerns and reinforce high cross-directory coupling.
evidence: holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles: src/shared/hooks/ has 95 files; src/shared/components/ has 78 files with mixed roles.
holistic_context.structure.coupling_matrix highlights heavy shared coupling (src/shared/hooks/ → src/shared/lib/ 62; src/shared/components/ → src/shared/hooks/ 56).
Migration utilities currently live beside presentational components in src/shared/components/.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 create target folders `src/domains/segment-settings/{migration,model,ui}`, `src/domains/travel-shot-editor/{controllers,services,state}`, and `src/platform/error-handling`; Stage 2 move files in order (first pure utilities like segmentSettingsMigration*.ts, then controller modules, then shared error handling adapters); Stage 3 run import updates with `rg -l "@/shared/components/segmentSettings" src | xargs sed -i '' -e 's#@/shared/components/segmentSettings#@/domains/segment-settings#g'` (and equivalent targeted replacements), then validate with `pnpm typecheck && pnpm test -w`.
